content.text: is a REST API that lets organisation administrators programmatically manage every resource that would otherwise require clicking through the Anthropic Console: users, workspaces, API keys, usage data, audit logs, and billing. Automating these operations is essential for organisations that onboard hundreds of members, rotate keys on a schedule, or need to feed usage data into internal BI tools.

This document covers authentication, the resource model, core endpoints, pagination, error handling, and practical Python patterns — everything you need to make your first successful Admin API call and integrate it into operational workflows.
alongside the inference API, but requires an
rather than a regular key. Every request touches org-level data — the key is always scoped to the organisation, not to an individual user or workspace.
— read org metadata, update display name and settings.
— create, rename, deactivate, and list workspaces within the org.
— invite users by email, assign roles (admin / developer / billing), remove members.
— create workspace-scoped keys, list active keys, revoke compromised or rotated keys.
— query token consumption per workspace, model, or time window.
— stream NDJSON event records for compliance pipelines.
The Console UI and the Admin API share the same underlying data — there is no separate admin back-end. Any change made via the API is immediately visible in the Console and vice versa.
Admin API keys look like regular Anthropic API keys but carry an elevated scope. Generate them in
Console → Organization Settings → API Keys → Create Admin Key
— never confuse them with ordinary keys (prefix

# Minimal request — check org identity
curl -s https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations \
  -H "x-api-key: $ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY" \
  -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" | jq .
(or the latest stable version). Mutating requests (POST, PATCH, DELETE) also need

import os
import anthropic

# The SDK auto-reads ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY from the environment
client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])

# Organisation details
org = client.admin.organizations.retrieve()
print(org.display_name, org.id)

Store the admin key in a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager) and inject it at runtime. Never hardcode it, never commit it, never log it. Rotate on a 90-day schedule at minimum.
The following table summarises the most-used endpoint groups. All paths are relative to the base URL.

import anthropic, os

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])

workspace = client.admin.workspaces.create(
    display_name="ml-research",
    description="Workspace for ML research team"
)
print(workspace.id)   # wkspc_01...
print(workspace.display_name)

# Invite with 'developer' role — valid roles: admin | developer | billing
invite = client.admin.invites.create(
    email="alice@company.com",
    role="developer",
    workspace_ids=["wkspc_01abc"]   # optional: pre-assign to workspaces
)
print(invite.status)   # "pending"
print(invite.expires_at)

# Create a workspace-scoped key for CI/CD
api_key = client.admin.api_keys.create(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    name="ci-cd-pipeline"
)
# api_key.key is ONLY shown once — store it immediately
secret = api_key.key
print(f"Created {api_key.name}: {secret[:20]}...")

# Later: revoke by key ID
client.admin.api_keys.delete(api_key_id=api_key.id)
print("Key revoked")
The raw key value is returned only on creation. Subsequent GET calls return only the key ID and metadata — not the secret. If you lose the key, you must delete the old one and create a new one.

. This is useful for recent-first audit log streams where you want to re-read only newly added events.
Admin API errors follow the same envelope as the inference API. HTTP status codes signal the category; the JSON body contains a machine-readable type and a human-readable message.
Missing field, wrong value type, or constraint violation
Key lacks admin scope, or role insufficient
Resource ID does not exist in this org
Duplicate name or email already exists
Too many requests — back off and retry
Transient Anthropic-side error — retry with backoff
endpoint aggregates token consumption across the organisation. Filter by workspace, model, or date range to produce chargeback reports or identify heavy users.

from datetime import date, timedelta

# Last 30 days of usage per workspace
today = date.today()
start = today - timedelta(days=30)

usage = client.admin.usage.retrieve(
    start_date=start.isoformat(),   # "YYYY-MM-DD"
    end_date=today.isoformat(),
    group_by="workspace",
    # model="claude-sonnet-4-6"    # optional: filter to a single model
)

for entry in usage.data:
    print(
        f"{entry.workspace_id}: "
        f"{entry.input_tokens:,} input + {entry.output_tokens:,} output tokens"
    )
The response can also be grouped by
groupings in a single call is not supported — run two separate queries and join them in your application layer.
Automated key rotation is one of the highest-value Admin API use cases. The workflow below shows a zero-downtime rotation pattern safe to run from a CI/CD pipeline or a scheduled Lambda.
and save it to the secrets manager.
to all consumers (environment variables, K8s secrets, parameter store).
— allow 1–5 minutes for rolling deployments to pick up the new value.
— run a low-cost inference call with the new key to confirm it works.

import anthropic, os, boto3

WORKSPACE_ID = os.environ["WORKSPACE_ID"]
SECRET_NAME  = os.environ["SECRET_NAME"]   # e.g. "/prod/claude/api-key"

admin = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])
ssm   = boto3.client("ssm")

def rotate_workspace_key():
    # 1. Find current key ID (stored alongside secret)
    old_meta = ssm.get_parameter(Name=f"{SECRET_NAME}/id", WithDecryption=False)
    old_key_id = old_meta["Parameter"]["Value"]

    # 2. Create new key
    new_key = admin.admin.api_keys.create(
        workspace_id=WORKSPACE_ID,
        name="auto-rotated"
    )

    # 3. Store in Parameter Store
    ssm.put_parameter(Name=SECRET_NAME, Value=new_key.key,
                      Type="SecureString", Overwrite=True)
    ssm.put_parameter(Name=f"{SECRET_NAME}/id", Value=new_key.id,
                      Type="String", Overwrite=True)

    # 4. Propagation window
    import time; time.sleep(300)

    # 5. Revoke old key
    admin.admin.api_keys.delete(api_key_id=old_key_id)
    print(f"Rotated: {old_key_id} -> {new_key.id}")

rotate_workspace_key()

Admin API requests share a separate quota from inference calls. Anthropic currently imposes limits per organisation per minute; the exact values are not published but are generous enough for operational scripts.
are read-heavy and rate-limited more loosely than mutating endpoints.
(invite, delete, key creation) have tighter per-minute limits — batch your operations and space them with a small sleep between iterations.
with exponential backoff starting at 1 second. The
header indicates minimum wait time when present.
Separate admin keys from inference keys.
Issue one admin key per automation pipeline and scope inference keys to individual workspaces.
Log every Admin API call in your own SIEM — Anthropic's audit log is not a substitute for your own records.
Org-level admin keys have broad blast radius. Create workspace-scoped keys for day-to-day inference.
Assign the billing role only to finance; assign admin only to IT leads; use developer for everyone else.
Create a non-production workspace and run Admin API scripts against it before targeting the production workspace.
Schedule a weekly job that lists all API keys and flags any that are older than 90 days for rotation.
The Claude Admin API converts manual Console tasks into automatable REST operations. Authenticate with an admin-scoped key (

, and navigate resources through cursor-based list calls. Key operations — inviting members, creating workspace-scoped API keys, querying token usage — all follow the same request/response shape. Layer in exponential-backoff retry for 429 and 5xx responses, store admin keys in a secrets manager, and rotate on a schedule to maintain a secure, self-managing Claude deployment.
